title: Gustar — saying what you like
grammar_notes: 'gustar' literally means 'to please'. Spanish says 'the book pleases me', not 'I like the book': Me gusta el libro.
The thing(s) liked is the SUBJECT — so verb agrees with it: Me gustan los libros (the books please me).
Russian works the same way: 'мне нравится книга' / 'мне нравятся книги' — exactly the same logic.
Same pattern for encantar (love), interesar (interest), doler (hurt). Me duele la cabeza = my head hurts.
example_phrases: Me gusta el chocolate. (I like chocolate.)
¿Te gustan los gatos? (Do you like cats?)
